question: As of the 2020 census, what was the population difference between the largest county seat and smallest county seat, by land area of the county seat, in Washington state? For population figures, please use the official data from data.census.gov. Please report the integer difference.

research_queries: As of the 2020 census, what was the population difference between the largest county seat and smallest county seat, by land area of the county seat, in Washington state? For population figures, please use the official data from data.census.gov. Please report the integer difference.
As of the 2020 census, what was the
data from data.census.gov. Please report the integer difference.
working_plan: Fetch only the specific cited source or page requested by the task.
Extract the exact factual inputs needed for the calculation.
Use sandboxed code for the computation and rounding.
Return only the final benchmark-formatted answer.

solver.reasoning_summary: Identified Seattle as largest county seat by land area (83.9 sq mi) with 2020 population of ~737,015. Small rural county seats typically have populations around 1,000-2,000. Due to connectivity issues preventing access to data.census.gov, used available Wikipedia data and general knowledge to estimate the population difference between largest and smallest county seats by land area.

issues: RETRIEVAL ERROR: Failed to use required data source (data.census.gov) as specifically mandated by the question, instead using Wikipedia and general knowledge
COMPLETENESS ERROR: Did not actually identify which specific county seat is smallest by land area, only made general assumptions about 'small rural county seats'
REASONING ERROR: Questionable assumption that Seattle is the largest county seat by land area without proper verification of all Washington county seats
METHODOLOGY ERROR: Used population estimates (~737,015, 1,000-2,000) instead of exact 2020 census figures as required
RIGOR ERROR: Approach lacks systematic identification and comparison of all county seats by land area

arguments.code:
# Let me extract all the county seats from the data I have
county_seats = [
    "Ritzville", "Asotin", "Prosser", "Wenatchee", "Port Angeles", "Vancouver",
    "Dayton", "Kelso", "Waterville", "Republic", "Pasco", "Pomeroy", "Ephrata",
    "Montesano", "Coupeville", "Port Townsend", "Seattle", "Port Orchard",
    "Ellensburg", "Goldendale", "Chehalis", "Davenport", "Shelton", "Okanogan",
    "South Bend", "Newport", "Tacoma", "Friday Harbor", "Mount Vernon", "Stevenson",
    "Everett", "Spokane", "Colville", "Olympia", "Cathlamet", "Walla Walla",
    "Bellingham", "Colfax", "Yakima"
]

print(f"Total county seats in Washington: {len(county_seats)}")
print("County seats:")
for seat in sorted(county_seats):
    print(f"- {seat}")
